title: Korean Milk Loaf

overview.description: A soft, lightly sweet Korean-style milk loaf made entirely in a bread maker. This version uses liquid milk rather than milk powder or other milk solids.

method: Add the ingredients to the bread maker in the order recommended by the manufacturer.
Select the Basic or White Bread programme, preferably with a light crust setting.
Allow the bread maker to complete the kneading, proving and baking cycle.
Remove the loaf from the bread maker and allow it to cool before slicing.

learning.did-you-know: Korean milk bread is usually made with a very soft, enriched dough and is known for its fluffy, tender texture. A bread maker produces the same style of dough and flavour, although without the traditional pull-apart shaping.
learning.what-you-will-learn: How liquid milk, butter and egg create a soft, rich bread without using milk powder or other milk solids.

notes.why-its-in-the-chapman-kitchen: A soft, lightly sweet everyday loaf that makes a good breakfast bread and can be made almost entirely hands-off in the bread maker.
chapman-tips: Add the ingredients in the order recommended by your bread maker manufacturer.
Because the loaf contains milk, egg and butter, it is richer and softer than a standard white loaf.
Allow the loaf to cool before slicing so the crumb has time to set.
The loaf is especially good toasted after the first day.
notes.batch-cooking: Freeze sliced portions so individual slices can be removed as needed.
notes.leftovers: Excellent toasted, used for French toast or turned into breadcrumbs once stale.
